questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 上託管多人遊戲應用程式。該公司希望應用程式能夠以亞毫秒延遲讀取資料並對歷史資料運行一次性查詢。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts a multiplayer gaming application on AWS. The company wants the application to read data with sub-millisecond latency and run one-time queries on historical data. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 將 Amazon RDS 用於經常存取的資料。定期執行自訂腳本以將資料匯出至 Amazon S3 儲存桶。
A.en: Use Amazon RDS for data that is frequently accessed. Run a periodic custom script to export the data to an Amazon S3 bucket.

B.zh: 將資料直接儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。實施 S3 生命週期策略，將舊資料移至 S3 Glacier Deep Archive 進行長期儲存。使用 Amazon Athena 對 Amazon S3 中的資料執行一次性查詢。
B.en: Store the data directly in an Amazon S3 bucket. Implement an S3 Lifecycle policy to move older data to S3 Glacier Deep Archive for long-term storage. Run one-time queries on the data in Amazon S3 by using Amazon Athena.

C.zh: 將 Amazon DynamoDB 與 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 結合使用來處理經常存取的資料。使用 DynamoDB 表格匯出將資料匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用 Amazon Athena 對 Amazon S3 中的資料執行一次性查詢。
C.en: Use Amazon DynamoDB with DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) for data that is frequently accessed. Export the data to an Amazon S3 bucket by using DynamoDB table export. Run one-time queries on the data in Amazon S3 by using Amazon Athena.

D.zh: 將 Amazon DynamoDB 用於經常存取的資料。開啟到 Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 的串流。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 從 Kinesis Data Streams 讀取資料。將記錄儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。
D.en: Use Amazon DynamoDB for data that is frequently accessed. Turn on streaming to Amazon Kinesis Data Streams. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to read the data from Kinesis Data Streams. Store the records in an Amazon S3 bucket.

A.zh: 錯誤。使用 Amazon RDS 儲存經常存取的資料。執行計劃的自訂腳本以將資料匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。 Amazon RDS雖然可以儲存頻繁存取的數據，但其查詢效能無法滿足「亞秒級」的要求。另外，定期匯出資料到S3增加了操作的複雜度，不符合「最小操作開銷」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Use Amazon RDS to store frequently accessed data. Run a scheduled custom script to export data to an Amazon S3 bucket. Although Amazon RDS can store frequently accessed data, its query performance cannot meet the "sub-second" requirements. In addition, regularly exporting data to S3 increases the complexity of the operation and does not meet the requirement of "minimum operation overhead".

B.zh: 錯誤。此選項Amazon S3適合儲存大量數據，但無法滿足「幾秒」的要求。 Athena適合執行單一查詢，但無法提供即時存取功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Store data directly in an Amazon S3 bucket. Implement an S3 lifecycle policy to move older data to S3 Glacier Deep Archive for long-term storage. Use Amazon Athena to run one-time queries against data in Amazon S3. Amazon S3 is suitable for storing large amounts of data, but it cannot meet the requirements of "several seconds". Athena is suitable for running a single query, but it cannot provide real-time access capabilities.

C.zh: 正確。使用 Amazon DynamoDB 和 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 儲存經常存取的資料。透過 DynamoDB 表格匯出功能將資料匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用 Amazon Athena 對 Amazon S3 中的資料執行一次性查詢。 Amazon DynamoDB 與 DAX 結合可以提供「亞秒級」的資料存取能力。 DynamoDB 表格匯出功能可以輕鬆將資料匯出到 S3，並且 Athena 可以針對 S3 中的歷史資料有效地執行單一查詢。這種架構設計滿足了低延遲和歷史資料查詢的需求，同時運行開銷較低。
C.en: Correct. Use Amazon DynamoDB and DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) to store frequently accessed data. Export data to an Amazon S3 bucket via the DynamoDB table export feature. Use Amazon Athena to run one-time queries against data in Amazon S3. Amazon DynamoDB combined with DAX can provide "sub-second" data access capabilities. DynamoDB table export functionality makes it easy to export data to S3, and Athena can efficiently run a single query against historical data in S3. This architectural design meets the needs of low latency and historical data query, and at the same time has low operating overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。使用 Amazon DynamoDB 儲存經常存取的資料。啟用至 Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 的串流。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 從 Kinesis Data Streams 讀取資料。將記錄儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。雖然DynamoDB可以提供低延遲的訪問，但是Kinesis Data Streams和Kinesis Data Firehose的引入增加了架構的複雜性和操作開銷，並不能滿足「最小操作開銷」的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Use Amazon DynamoDB to store frequently accessed data. Enable streaming to Amazon Kinesis Data Streams. Read data from Kinesis Data Streams using Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose. Store records in an Amazon S3 bucket. Although DynamoDB can provide low-latency access, the introduction of Kinesis Data Streams and Kinesis Data Firehose increases the complexity of the architecture and operation overhead, and does not meet the requirement of "minimum operation overhead".

answer_text.zh: C. 將 Amazon DynamoDB 與 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 結合使用來處理經常存取的資料。使用 DynamoDB 表格匯出將資料匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用 Amazon Athena 對 Amazon S3 中的資料執行一次性查詢。
answer_text.en: C. Use Amazon DynamoDB with DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) for data that is frequently accessed. Export the data to an Amazon S3 bucket by using DynamoDB table export. Run one-time queries on the data in Amazon S3 by using Amazon Athena.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：使用 Amazon DynamoDB 和 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 儲存經常存取的資料。透過 DynamoDB 表格匯出功能將資料匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用 Amazon Athena 對 Amazon S3 中的資料執行一次性查詢。 Amazon DynamoDB 與 DAX 結合可以提供「亞秒級」的資料存取能力。 DynamoDB 表格匯出功能可以輕鬆將資料匯出到 S3，並且 Athena 可以針對 S3 中的歷史資料有效地執行單一查詢。這種架構設計滿足了低延遲和歷史資料查詢的需求，同時運行開銷較低。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Use Amazon DynamoDB and DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) to store frequently accessed data. Export data to an Amazon S3 bucket via the DynamoDB table export feature. Use Amazon Athena to run one-time queries against data in Amazon S3. Amazon DynamoDB combined with DAX can provide "sub-second" data access capabilities. DynamoDB table export functionality makes it easy to export data to S3, and Athena can efficiently run a single query against historical data in S3. This architectural design meets the needs of low latency and historical data query, and at the same time has low operating overhead.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用的支付處理系統要求依照傳送順序接收特定支付 ID 的訊息。否則，付款可能會被錯誤處理。解決方案架構師應該採取哪些行動來滿足此要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A company uses a payment processing system that requires messages for a particular payment ID to be received in the same order that they were sent. Otherwise, the payments might be processed incorrectly. Which actions should a solutions architect take to meet this requirement? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 將訊息寫入 Amazon DynamoDB 表，並使用付款 ID 作為分區鍵。
A.en: Write the messages to an Amazon DynamoDB table with the payment ID as the partition key.

B.zh: 將訊息寫入 Amazon Kinesis 資料流，並使用付款 ID 作為分區鍵。
B.en: Write the messages to an Amazon Kinesis data stream with the payment ID as the partition key.

C.zh: 使用付款 ID 作為鍵將訊息寫入 Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached 叢集。
C.en: Write the messages to an Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached cluster with the payment ID as the key.

D.zh: 將訊息寫入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 佇列。設定訊息屬性以使用付款 ID。
D.en: Write the messages to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Set the message attribute to use the payment ID.

E.zh: 將訊息寫入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO 佇列。設定訊息組以使用付款 ID。
E.en: Write the messages to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue. Set the message group to use the payment ID.

A.zh: 錯誤。DynamoDB 是一種 NoSQL 資料庫。雖然分區鍵可以用來組織數據，但它不能保證訊息的順序。
A.en: Incorrect. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database. Although partition keys can be used to organize data, it does not guarantee the order of messages.

B.zh: 正確。Kinesis資料流支援分區鍵，可確保同一分區內訊息的順序。
B.en: Correct. Kinesis data stream supports partition keys, which can ensure the order of messages within the same partition.

C.zh: 錯誤。ElastiCache for Memcached 是一種記憶體快取服務，不適合處理訊息佇列或保證訊息順序。
C.en: Incorrect. ElastiCache for Memcached is an in-memory caching service that is not suitable for handling message queues or guaranteeing message order.

D.zh: 錯誤。普通的SQS佇列不保證訊息的順序，即使設定了訊息屬性。
D.en: Incorrect. Ordinary SQS queues do not guarantee the order of messages, even if message attributes are set.

E.zh: 正確。SQS FIFO佇列支援訊息組，可以保證同一組內訊息的順序。
E.en: Correct. SQS FIFO queue supports message groups, which can ensure the order of messages within the same group.

answer_text.zh: B. 將訊息寫入 Amazon Kinesis 資料流，並使用付款 ID 作為分區鍵。；E. 將訊息寫入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO 佇列。設定訊息組以使用付款 ID。
answer_text.en: B. Write the messages to an Amazon Kinesis data stream with the payment ID as the partition key.; E. Write the messages to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue. Set the message group to use the payment ID.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：BE (80%)； AE (17%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Kinesis資料流支援分區鍵，可確保同一分區內訊息的順序。；SQS FIFO佇列支援訊息組，可以保證同一組內訊息的順序。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BE (80%); AE (17%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Kinesis data stream supports partition keys, which can ensure the order of messages within the same partition.; SQS FIFO queue supports message groups, which can ensure the order of messages within the same group.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在建立一個遊戲系統，該系統需要同時向單獨的排行榜、匹配和身份驗證服務發送獨特的事件。該公司需要一個 AWS 事件驅動系統來保證事件的順序。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is building a game system that needs to send unique events to separate leaderboard, matchmaking, and authentication services concurrently. The company needs an AWS event-driven system that guarantees the order of the events. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: Amazon EventBridge 事件匯流排
A.en: Amazon EventBridge event bus

B.zh: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO 主題
B.en: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO topics

C.zh: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 標準主題
C.en: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) standard topics

D.zh: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO 佇列
D.en: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queues

A.zh: 錯誤。EventBridge 是一種事件匯流排服務，可用於事件驅動架構，但它不保證事件的順序。因此，該選項不符合問題中「保證事件發生順序」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. EventBridge is an event bus service that can be used in event-driven architectures, but it does not guarantee the order of events. Therefore, this option does not meet the requirement of "guaranteing the order of events" in the question.

B.zh: 正確。SNS FIFO 主題支援事件的順序性質，並且可以將事件分發給多個訂閱者（例如服務）。此選項滿足問題中「保證事件的順序」和「分發到多個服務」的要求。
B.en: Correct. SNS FIFO topics support the sequential nature of events and can distribute events to multiple subscribers (such as services). This option meets the requirements of "guaranteing the order of events" and "distributing to multiple services" in the question.

C.zh: 錯誤。SNS 標準主題支援向多個訂閱者分發事件，但不保證事件的順序。因此，該選項不符合問題中「保證事件發生順序」的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. SNS standard topics support event distribution to multiple subscribers, but do not guarantee the order of events. Therefore, this option does not meet the requirement of "guaranteing the order of events" in the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。SQS FIFO佇列支援事件的順序，但它是點對點的訊息佇列服務，不能將事件分發到多個服務。因此，該選項不符合問題中「分發到多個服務」的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. SQS FIFO queue supports the sequence of events, but it is a point-to-point message queue service and cannot distribute events to multiple services. Therefore, this option does not meet the requirement of "distribute to multiple services" in the question.

answer_text.zh: B. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO 主題
answer_text.en: B. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO topics

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (79%)； D (16%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：SNS FIFO 主題支援事件的順序性質，並且可以將事件分發給多個訂閱者（例如服務）。此選項滿足問題中「保證事件的順序」和「分發到多個服務」的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (79%); D (16%). Popular discussion and key point summary: SNS FIFO topics support the sequential nature of events and can distribute events to multiple subscribers (such as services). This option meets the requirements of "guaranteing the order of events" and "distributing to multiple services" in the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家醫院正在設計一種新的應用程序，用於收集患者的症狀。該醫院決定在架構中使用 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 和 Amazon Simple notification Service (Amazon SNS)。解決方案架構師正在審查基礎設施設計。資料在靜態和傳輸過程中必須進行加密。只有醫院的授權人員才能存取資料。解決方案架構師應該採取哪些步驟組合來滿足這些要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A hospital is designing a new application that gathers symptoms from patients. The hospital has decided to use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) and Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) in the architecture. A solutions architect is reviewing the infrastructure design. Data must be encrypted at rest and in transit. Only authorized personnel of the hospital should be able to access the data. Which combination of steps should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 在 SQS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。更新預設金鑰策略以將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。
A.en: Turn on server-side encryption on the SQS components. Update the default key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客戶託管金鑰在 SNS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。應用金鑰策略將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。
B.en: Turn on server-side encryption on the SNS components by using an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Apply a key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals.

C.zh: 在 SNS 元件上啟用加密。更新預設金鑰策略以將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。在主題策略中設定條件以僅允許透過 TLS 進行加密連線。
C.en: Turn on encryption on the SNS components. Update the default key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals. Set a condition in the topic policy to allow only encrypted connections over TLS.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客戶託管金鑰在 SQS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。應用金鑰策略將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。在佇列策略中設定條件以僅允許透過 TLS 的加密連線。
D.en: Turn on server-side encryption on the SQS components by using an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Apply a key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals. Set a condition in the queue policy to allow only encrypted connections over TLS.

E.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客戶託管金鑰在 SQS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。應用 IAM 策略將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。在佇列策略中設定條件以僅允許透過 TLS 的加密連線。
E.en: Turn on server-side encryption on the SQS components by using an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Apply an IAM policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals. Set a condition in the queue policy to allow only encrypted connections over TLS.

A.zh: 錯誤。啟用伺服器端加密是正確的，但使用認證加密策略可能不夠安全。該問題需要使用客戶管理的密鑰而不是識別密鑰。
A.en: Incorrect. Enabling server-side encryption is correct, but using the authentication encryption policy may not be secure enough. The question requires the use of a Customer Managed Key instead of a recognition key.

B.zh: 正確。客戶管理的 KMS 金鑰對靜態 SNS 訊息進行加密，其金鑰策略可限制授權醫院負責人使用該金鑰。
B.en: Correct. A customer managed KMS key encrypts SNS messages at rest, and its key policy can restrict use of the key to authorized hospital principals.

C.zh: 錯誤。啟用加密是正確的，但問題需要使用客戶管理的金鑰而不是已識別的金鑰。設定允許加密連線的條件是正確的，但此選項沒有提及使用 KMS 用戶端託管的金鑰。
C.en: Incorrect. Enabling encryption is correct, but the question requires the use of customer-managed keys instead of recognized keys. Setting conditions to allow encrypted connections is correct, but this option makes no mention of using KMS client-hosted keys.

D.zh: 正確。客戶管理的 KMS 金鑰對靜態 SQS 訊息進行加密，其金鑰策略限制金鑰使用，且佇列策略可能需要 TLS 進行傳輸加密。
D.en: Correct. A customer managed KMS key encrypts SQS messages at rest, its key policy restricts key use, and the queue policy can require TLS for encryption in transit.

E.zh: 錯誤。啟用伺服器端加密並使用客戶託管的加密滿足問題要求。但與使用金鑰策略相比，使用 IAM 策略來限制金鑰使用的安全性和一致性較差。
E.en: Incorrect. Enabling server-side encryption and using customer-hosted encryption meets the question requirements. But using IAM policies to restrict key usage is less secure and consistent with best practices than using Key Policy.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客戶託管金鑰在 SNS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。應用金鑰策略將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。；D. 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客戶託管金鑰在 SQS 元件上開啟伺服器端加密。應用金鑰策略將金鑰的使用限制為一組授權主體。在佇列策略中設定條件以僅允許透過 TLS 的加密連線。
answer_text.en: B. Turn on server-side encryption on the SNS components by using an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Apply a key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals.; D. Turn on server-side encryption on the SQS components by using an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Apply a key policy to restrict key usage to a set of authorized principals. Set a condition in the queue policy to allow only encrypted connections over TLS.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：BD (71%)； CD (18%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：客戶管理的 KMS 金鑰對靜態 SNS 訊息進行加密，其金鑰策略可限制授權醫院負責人使用該金鑰。；客戶管理的 KMS 金鑰對靜態 SQS 訊息進行加密，其金鑰策略限制金鑰使用，且佇列策略可能需要 TLS 進行傳輸加密。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BD (71%); CD (18%). Popular discussion and key point summary: A customer managed KMS key encrypts SNS messages at rest, and its key policy can restrict use of the key to authorized hospital principals.; A customer managed KMS key encrypts SQS messages at rest, its key policy restricts key use, and the queue policy can require TLS for encryption in transit.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司運行由 Amazon RDS 支援的 Web 應用程式。一位新的資料庫管理員因意外編輯資料庫表中的資訊而導致資料遺失。為了幫助從此類事件中恢復，該公司希望能夠將資料庫恢復到過去 30 天內發生任何更改之前 5 分鐘的狀態。解決方案架構師應該在設計中包含哪些功能來滿足此要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a web application that is backed by Amazon RDS. A new database administrator caused data loss by accidentally editing information in a database table. To help recover from this type of incident, the company wants the ability to restore the database to its state from 5 minutes before any change within the last 30 days. Which feature should the solutions architect include in the design to meet this requirement?

A.zh: 讀取副本
A.en: Read replicas

B.zh: 手動快照
B.en: Manual snapshots

C.zh: 自動備份
C.en: Automated backups

D.zh: 多可用區部署
D.en: Multi-AZ deployments

A.zh: 錯誤。只讀副本用於提高資料庫的讀取效能，分擔主庫的負載，但不提供資料恢復到特定時間點的功能。
A.en: Incorrect. The read replica is used to improve the read performance of the database and share the load of the primary database, but it does not provide the function of data recovery to a specific point in time.

B.zh: 錯誤。此選項是使用者手動建立的資料庫備份，可用於還原資料。但手動快照無法滿足問題中「恢復到5分鐘前的狀態」的要求，因為它需要使用者主動建立快照，無法提供持續的時間點復原功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Manual snapshot is a database backup manually created by the user, which can be used to restore data. However, manual snapshots cannot meet the requirement of "restoring to the state 5 minutes ago" in the question, because it requires users to actively create snapshots and cannot provide continuous point-in-time recovery functionality.

C.zh: 正確。此選項是Amazon RDS提供的一項功能，支援連續時間點復原（Point-in-Time Recovery），可還原至過去30天內的任意時間點。這完全符合題目要求。
C.en: Correct. Automatic backup is a function provided by Amazon RDS, which supports continuous point-in-time recovery (Point-in-Time Recovery) and can be restored to any point in time within the past 30 days. This fully meets the question requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。多AZ部署用於提高資料庫的高可用和容錯能力，但不提供資料恢復到特定時間點的功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Multi-AZ deployment is used to improve the high availability and fault tolerance of the database, but it does not provide the function of data recovery to a specific point in time.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：此選項是Amazon RDS提供的一項功能，支援連續時間點復原（Point-in-Time Recovery），可還原至過去30天內的任意時間點。這完全符合題目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Automatic backup is a function provided by Amazon RDS, which supports continuous point-in-time recovery (Point-in-Time Recovery) and can be restored to any point in time within the past 30 days. This fully meets the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司的 Web 應用程式由 AWS Lambda 函數前面的 Amazon API Gateway API 和 Amazon DynamoDB 資料庫組成。 Lambda 函數處理業務邏輯，DynamoDB 資料表託管資料。該應用程式使用 Amazon Cognito 用戶池來識別應用程式的各個用戶。解決方案架構師需要更新應用程序，以便只有訂閱的用戶才能存取優質內容。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這項要求？
question_text.en: A company’s web application consists of an Amazon API Gateway API in front of an AWS Lambda function and an Amazon DynamoDB database. The Lambda function handles the business logic, and the DynamoDB table hosts the data. The application uses Amazon Cognito user pools to identify the individual users of the application. A solutions architect needs to update the application so that only users who have a subscription can access premium content. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 在 API Gateway API 上啟用 API 快取和限制。
A.en: Enable API caching and throttling on the API Gateway API.

B.zh: 在 API Gateway API 上設定 AWS WAF。建立規則來過濾擁有訂閱的使用者。
B.en: Set up AWS WAF on the API Gateway API. Create a rule to filter users who have a subscription.

C.zh: 將細粒度 IAM 權限套用至 DynamoDB 表中的進階內容。
C.en: Apply fine-grained IAM permissions to the premium content in the DynamoDB table.

D.zh: 實作 API 使用計畫和 API 金鑰，以限制沒有訂閱的使用者的存取。
D.en: Implement API usage plans and API keys to limit the access of users who do not have a subscription.

A.zh: 錯誤。API快取和限流主要用於最佳化效能和控制流量，而不是限制使用者存取特定內容。此選項無法滿足「僅限訂閱者存取優質內容」的需求。
A.en: Incorrect. API caching and flow limiting are mainly used to optimize performance and control traffic, rather than restricting users from accessing specific content. This option does not satisfy the need for "subscriber-only access to premium content".

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS WAF 主要用於保護 API 免受惡意流量攻擊，不用於基於使用者訂閱狀態的存取控制。建立規則來過濾訂閱用戶既複雜又昂貴。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS WAF is primarily used to protect APIs from malicious traffic attacks and is not used for access control based on user subscription status. Creating rules to filter subscribed users is complex and expensive.

C.zh: 錯誤。IAM 權限可以控制對 DynamoDB 表的訪問，但無法直接根據使用者訂閱狀態進行動態控制。實現此功能需要額外的邏輯和操作開銷。
C.en: Incorrect. IAM permissions can control access to DynamoDB tables, but cannot be dynamically controlled directly based on user subscription status. Implementing this functionality requires additional logic and operational overhead.

D.zh: 正確。API使用計畫和API金鑰是API閘道的內建功能，可輕鬆實現基於訂閱狀態的存取控制。此方案運作開銷最低，直接滿足問題的需求。
D.en: Correct. API usage plans and API keys are built-in features of API Gateway, making it easy to implement access control based on subscription status. This solution has the lowest operating overhead and directly meets the needs of the question.

answer_text.zh: D. 實作 API 使用計畫和 API 金鑰，以限制沒有訂閱的使用者的存取。
answer_text.en: D. Implement API usage plans and API keys to limit the access of users who do not have a subscription.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (86%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：API使用計畫和API金鑰是API閘道的內建功能，可輕鬆實現基於訂閱狀態的存取控制。此方案運作開銷最低，直接滿足問題的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (86%). Popular discussion and key point summary: API usage plans and API keys are built-in features of API Gateway, making it easy to implement access control based on subscription status. This solution has the lowest operating overhead and directly meets the needs of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在使用 Amazon Route 53 基於延遲的路由將世界各地用戶的請求路由到其基於 UDP 的應用程式。該應用程式託管在該公司位於美國、亞洲和歐洲的本地資料中心的冗餘伺服器上。該公司的合規性要求規定應用程式必須在本地託管。該公司希望提高應用程式的效能和可用性。解決方案架構師應該怎麼做才能滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is using Amazon Route 53 latency-based routing to route requests to its UDP-based application for users around the world. The application is hosted on redundant servers in the company's on-premises data centers in the United States, Asia, and Europe. The company’s compliance requirements state that the application must be hosted on premises. The company wants to improve the performance and availability of the application. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在三個 AWS 區域中配置三個網路負載平衡器 (NLB) 以定址本機終端節點。使用 AWS Global Accelerator 建立加速器，並將 NLB 註冊為其終端節點。使用指向加速器 DNS 的 CNAME 提供對應用程式的存取。
A.en: Configure three Network Load Balancers (NLBs) in the three AWS Regions to address the on-premises endpoints. Create an accelerator by using AWS Global Accelerator, and register the NLBs as its endpoints. Provide access to the application by using a CNAME that points to the accelerator DNS.

B.zh: 在三個 AWS 區域中設定三個 Application Load Balancer (ALB) 以處理本機終端節點。使用 AWS Global Accelerator 建立加速器，並將 ALB 註冊為其終端節點。使用指向加速器 DNS 的 CNAME 提供對應用程式的存取。
B.en: Configure three Application Load Balancers (ALBs) in the three AWS Regions to address the on-premises endpoints. Create an accelerator by using AWS Global Accelerator, and register the ALBs as its endpoints. Provide access to the application by using a CNAME that points to the accelerator DNS.

C.zh: 在三個 AWS 區域中配置三個網路負載平衡器 (NLB) 以定址本機終端節點。在 Route 53 中，建立指向三個 NLB 的基於延遲的記錄，並將其用作 Amazon CloudFront 分配的來源。使用指向 CloudFront DNS 的 CNAME 提供對應用程式的存取。
C.en: Configure three Network Load Balancers (NLBs) in the three AWS Regions to address the on-premises endpoints. In Route 53, create a latency-based record that points to the three NLBs, and use it as an origin for an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Provide access to the application by using a CNAME that points to the CloudFront DNS.

D.zh: 在三個 AWS 區域中配置三個 Application Load Balancer (ALB) 以處理本地終端節點。在 Route 53 中，建立指向三個 ALB 的基於延遲的記錄，並將其用作 Amazon CloudFront 分配的來源。使用指向 CloudFront DNS 的 CNAME 提供對應用程式的存取。
D.en: Configure three Application Load Balancers (ALBs) in the three AWS Regions to address the on-premises endpoints. In Route 53, create a latency-based record that points to the three ALBs, and use it as an origin for an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Provide access to the application by using a CNAME that points to the CloudFront DNS.

A.zh: 正確。使用NLB可以處理UDP流量，滿足問題的要求。 AWS Global Accelerator 可優化跨區域的效能和可用性，並支援 UDP 協定。透過 CNAME 指向加速器 DNS 是正確的設定方法。此選項完全滿足題目要求。
A.en: Correct. Using NLB can handle UDP traffic, which meets the requirements of the question. AWS Global Accelerator optimizes performance and availability across regions and supports UDP protocol. Pointing to the accelerator DNS via CNAME is the correct way to configure it. This option fully meets the question requirements.

B.zh: 錯誤。ALB不支援UDP流量，僅支援HTTP/HTTPS協議，因此不符合問題的要求。雖然 Global Accelerator 可以優化效能，但 ALB 的協定限制使得此選項成為可能。
B.en: Incorrect. ALB does not support UDP traffic and only supports HTTP/HTTPS protocols, so it does not meet the requirements of the question. While Global Accelerator can optimize performance, ALB's protocol limitations make this option.

C.zh: 錯誤。雖然 NLB 支援 UDP 流量，但 CloudFront 僅支援 HTTP/HTTPS 協議，不支援 UDP 流量。 Route 53的延遲路由可以優化效能，但其與CloudFront結合使用並不能滿足問題的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Although NLB supports UDP traffic, CloudFront only supports HTTP/HTTPS protocols and does not support UDP traffic. Route 53's delayed routing can optimize performance, but its use in combination with CloudFront does not meet the requirements of the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。ALB不支援UDP流量，僅支援HTTP/HTTPS協議，因此不符合問題要求。 CloudFront 也不支援 UDP 流量。
D.en: Incorrect. ALB does not support UDP traffic and only supports HTTP/HTTPS protocols, so it does not meet the question requirements. CloudFront also doesn't support UDP traffic.

answer_text.zh: A. 在三個 AWS 區域中配置三個網路負載平衡器 (NLB) 以定址本機終端節點。使用 AWS Global Accelerator 建立加速器，並將 NLB 註冊為其終端節點。使用指向加速器 DNS 的 CNAME 提供對應用程式的存取。
answer_text.en: A. Configure three Network Load Balancers (NLBs) in the three AWS Regions to address the on-premises endpoints. Create an accelerator by using AWS Global Accelerator, and register the NLBs as its endpoints. Provide access to the application by using a CNAME that points to the accelerator DNS.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (98%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：使用NLB可以處理UDP流量，滿足問題的要求。 AWS Global Accelerator 可優化跨區域的效能和可用性，並支援 UDP 協定。透過 CNAME 指向加速器 DNS 是正確的設定方法。此選項完全滿足題目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (98%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Using NLB can handle UDP traffic, which meets the requirements of the question. AWS Global Accelerator optimizes performance and availability across regions and supports UDP protocol. Pointing to the accelerator DNS via CNAME is the correct way to configure it. This option fully meets the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師希望所有新使用者都有特定的複雜性需求和 IAM 使用者密碼的強制輪替週期。解決方案架構師應該做什麼來實現這個目標？
question_text.en: A solutions architect wants all new users to have specific complexity requirements and mandatory rotation periods for IAM user passwords. What should the solutions architect do to accomplish this?

A.zh: 為整個 AWS 帳戶設定整體密碼原則。
A.en: Set an overall password policy for the entire AWS account.

B.zh: 為 AWS 帳戶中的每個 IAM 使用者設定密碼原則。
B.en: Set a password policy for each IAM user in the AWS account.

C.zh: 使用第三方供應商軟體設定密碼要求。
C.en: Use third-party vendor software to set password requirements.

D.zh: 將 Amazon CloudWatch 規則附加到 Create_newuser 事件，以根據適當的要求設定密碼。
D.en: Attach an Amazon CloudWatch rule to the Create_newuser event to set the password with the appropriate requirements.

A.zh: 正確。AWS 允許在帳戶層級設定密碼策略，這可確保所有 IAM 使用者遵循統一的密碼複雜性要求和輪調策略。這是推薦的最佳實踐，因為它可以簡化管理並提高安全性。
A.en: Correct. AWS allows setting password policies at the account level, which ensures that all IAM users follow uniform password complexity requirements and rotation policies. This is a recommended best practice because it simplifies administration and improves security.

B.zh: 錯誤。。 AWS 不支援為單一 IAM 使用者設定獨立的密碼原則。密碼原則只能在帳戶層級設定。
B.en: Incorrect. . AWS does not support setting independent password policies for individual IAM users. Password policy can only be set at the account level.

C.zh: 錯誤。。雖然可以使用第三方工具來管理密碼，但這並不是AWS推薦的解決方案，也達不到問題「直接在AWS實現」的目的。
C.en: Incorrect. . Although you can use third-party tools to manage passwords, this is not a solution recommended by AWS, and it does not meet the purpose of the question "implement directly in AWS.".

D.zh: 錯誤。CloudWatch 或 EventBridge 規則無法定義 IAM 帳戶密碼策略，且對使用者建立事件的反應不會一致地強制密碼複雜性和輪替。
D.en: Incorrect. A CloudWatch or EventBridge rule cannot define an IAM account password policy, and reacting to user-creation events would not consistently enforce password complexity and rotation.

answer_text.zh: A. 為整個 AWS 帳戶設定整體密碼原則。
answer_text.en: A. Set an overall password policy for the entire AWS account.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (94%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS 允許在帳戶層級設定密碼策略，這可確保所有 IAM 使用者遵循統一的密碼複雜性要求和輪調策略。這是推薦的最佳實踐，因為它可以簡化管理並提高安全性。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (94%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS allows setting password policies at the account level, which ensures that all IAM users follow uniform password complexity requirements and rotation policies. This is a recommended best practice because it simplifies administration and improves security.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司已將應用程式移轉到 Amazon EC2 Linux 執行個體。其中一個 EC2 執行個體會依照計畫執行多個 1 小時的任務。這些任務是由不同的團隊編寫的，沒有通用的程式語言。該公司擔心這些任務在單一實例上運行時的效能和可擴展性。解決方案架構師需要實作一個解決方案來解決這些問題。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company has migrated an application to Amazon EC2 Linux instances. One of these EC2 instances runs several 1-hour tasks on a schedule. These tasks were written by different teams and have no common programming language. The company is concerned about performance and scalability while these tasks run on a single instance. A solutions architect needs to implement a solution to resolve these concerns. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Batch 將任務當作作業執行。使用 Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) 排程作業。
A.en: Use AWS Batch to run the tasks as jobs. Schedule the jobs by using Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events).

B.zh: 將 EC2 執行個體轉換為容器。使用 AWS App Runner 按需建立容器以將任務作為作業執行。
B.en: Convert the EC2 instance to a container. Use AWS App Runner to create the container on demand to run the tasks as jobs.

C.zh: 將任務複製到 AWS Lambda 函數中。使用 Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) 規劃 Lambda 函數。
C.en: Copy the tasks into AWS Lambda functions. Schedule the Lambda functions by using Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events).

D.zh: 建立執行任務的 EC2 執行個體的 Amazon 系統映像 (AMI)。使用 AMI 建立 Auto Scaling 群組以執行執行個體的多個副本。
D.en: Create an Amazon Machine Image (AMI) of the EC2 instance that runs the tasks. Create an Auto Scaling group with the AMI to run multiple copies of the instance.

A.zh: 正確。AWS Batch 是一項專門為執行批次任務而設計的服務。它支援多種程式語言和任務類型，可以高效管理資源並提供自動擴展能力。 Amazon EventBridge 可以輕鬆安排任務並減少營運開銷。此選項滿足效能和可擴展性要求，同時具有最低的營運開銷。
A.en: Correct. AWS Batch is a service specially designed for running batch tasks. It supports multiple programming languages ​​and task types, can manage resources efficiently and provide automatic expansion capabilities. Amazon EventBridge can easily schedule tasks and reduce operational overhead. this option meets performance and scalability requirements while having the lowest operational overhead.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS App Runner 主要用於執行 Web 應用程式或 API，而不是批次任務。將 EC2 執行個體轉換為容器需要額外的開發工作並增加營運開銷。這個選項不適合解決目前的問題。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS App Runner is primarily used to run web applications or APIs, not batch processing tasks. Converting EC2 instances to containers requires additional development work and adds operational overhead. this option is not suitable for solving the current problem.

C.zh: 錯誤。雖然 AWS Lambda 可以透過 EventBridge 執行任務並進行調度，但 Lambda 函數有運行時間限制（最長 15 分鐘），不適合執行 1 小時的任務。此外，將任務遷移到Lambda需要重寫程式碼，這會增加開發和運維開銷。
C.en: Incorrect. Although AWS Lambda can run tasks and schedule them through EventBridge, Lambda functions have a running time limit (maximum 15 minutes) and are not suitable for running tasks for 1 hour. In addition, migrating tasks to Lambda requires rewriting the code, which increases development and operational overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然Auto Scaling可以擴展EC2實例，但這種方法解決了任務調度的問題。運行多個實例會增加成本和管理複雜性，並且營運開銷很高。
D.en: Incorrect. Although Auto Scaling can expand EC2 instances, this method solves the problem of task scheduling. Running multiple instances increases cost and management complexity, and the operational overhead is high.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 AWS Batch 將任務當作作業執行。使用 Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) 排程作業。
answer_text.en: A. Use AWS Batch to run the tasks as jobs. Schedule the jobs by using Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events).

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (73%)； C (16%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS Batch 是一項專門為執行批次任務而設計的服務。它支援多種程式語言和任務類型，可以高效管理資源並提供自動擴展能力。 Amazon EventBridge 可以輕鬆安排任務並減少營運開銷。此選項滿足效能和可擴展性要求，同時具有最低的營運開銷。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (73%); C (16%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Batch is a service specially designed for running batch tasks. It supports multiple programming languages ​​and task types, can manage resources efficiently and provide automatic expansion capabilities. Amazon EventBridge can easily schedule tasks and reduce operational overhead. this option meets performance and scalability requirements while having the lowest operational overhead.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 VPC 中運行公共三層 Web 應用程式。該應用程式在跨多個可用區的 Amazon EC2 執行個體上執行。在私有子網路中執行的 EC2 執行個體需要透過 Internet 與許可證伺服器進行通訊。該公司需要一個可最大限度減少營運維護的託管解決方案。哪種解決方案滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a public three-tier web application in a VPC. The application runs on Amazon EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones. The EC2 instances that run in private subnets need to communicate with a license server over the internet. The company needs a managed solution that minimizes operational maintenance. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 在公有子網路中設定 NAT 實例。使用指向 NAT 實例的預設路由修改每個私有子網路的路由表。
A.en: Provision a NAT instance in a public subnet. Modify each private subnet's route table with a default route that points to the NAT instance.

B.zh: 在私有子網路中設定 NAT 實例。使用指向 NAT 實例的預設路由修改每個私有子網路的路由表。
B.en: Provision a NAT instance in a private subnet. Modify each private subnet's route table with a default route that points to the NAT instance.

C.zh: 在公用子網路中設定 NAT 閘道。使用指向 NAT 閘道的預設路由修改每個私有子網路的路由表。
C.en: Provision a NAT gateway in a public subnet. Modify each private subnet's route table with a default route that points to the NAT gateway.

D.zh: 在私有子網路中設定 NAT 閘道。使用指向 NAT 閘道的預設路由修改每個私有子網路的路由表。
D.en: Provision a NAT gateway in a private subnet. Modify each private subnet's route table with a default route that points to the NAT gateway.

A.zh: 錯誤。NAT實例可以實現私有子網路與網際網路的通信，但需要主動管理（如執行個體啟動、監控、擴充功能等），不符合「管理解決方案、減少維運工作」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. A NAT instance can realize communication between a private subnet and the Internet, but it requires active management (such as instance startup, monitoring, expansion, etc.), so it does not meet the requirements of "managing solutions and reducing operational work".

B.zh: 錯誤。NAT實例必須部署在公有子網路中才能存取Internet。如果部署在私人子網路中，則無法與 Internet 通信，因此該選項在技術上不可行。
B.en: Incorrect. The NAT instance must be deployed in a public subnet to access the Internet. If deployed in a private subnet, it cannot communicate with the Internet, so this option is not technically feasible.

C.zh: 正確。NAT網關是一項託管服務，AWS負責管理和維護。需要部署在公有子網路中，並透過路由表配置私有子網路的預設路由指向NAT網關。這是最佳實踐，符合問題的要求。
C.en: Correct. NAT gateway is a managed service, and AWS is responsible for management and maintenance. It needs to be deployed in the public subnet and configure the default route of the private subnet to point to the NAT gateway through the routing table. This is best practice and meets the requirements of the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。NAT閘道必須部署在公有子網路中才能存取Internet。如果部署在私人子網路中，則無法與 Internet 通信，因此該選項在技術上不可行。
D.en: Incorrect. The NAT gateway must be deployed in a public subnet to access the Internet. If deployed in a private subnet, it cannot communicate with the Internet, so this option is not technically feasible.

answer_text.zh: C. 在公用子網路中設定 NAT 閘道。使用指向 NAT 閘道的預設路由修改每個私有子網路的路由表。
answer_text.en: C. Provision a NAT gateway in a public subnet. Modify each private subnet's route table with a default route that points to the NAT gateway.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：NAT網關是一項託管服務，AWS負責管理和維護。需要部署在公有子網路中，並透過路由表配置私有子網路的預設路由指向NAT網關。這是最佳實踐，符合問題的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: NAT gateway is a managed service, and AWS is responsible for management and maintenance. It needs to be deployed in the public subnet and configure the default route of the private subnet to point to the NAT gateway through the routing table. This is best practice and meets the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要建立 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 叢集來託管數位媒體串流應用程式。 EKS 叢集將使用由 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區支援的託管節點群組進行儲存。公司必須使用儲存在 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 中的客戶託管金鑰對所有靜態資料進行加密。哪種操作組合能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足此要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A company needs to create an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster to host a digital media streaming application. The EKS cluster will use a managed node group that is backed by Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes for storage. The company must encrypt all data at rest by using a customer managed key that is stored in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Which combination of actions will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 使用 Kubernetes 插件，該插件使用客戶管理的金鑰來執行資料加密。
A.en: Use a Kubernetes plugin that uses the customer managed key to perform data encryption.

B.zh: 建立 EKS 叢集後，找到 EBS 磁碟區。使用客戶管理的密鑰啟用加密。
B.en: After creation of the EKS cluster, locate the EBS volumes. Enable encryption by using the customer managed key.

C.zh: 在將建立 EKS 叢集的 AWS 區域中預設啟用 EBS 加密。選擇客戶管理的密鑰作為預設密鑰。
C.en: Enable EBS encryption by default in the AWS Region where the EKS cluster will be created. Select the customer managed key as the default key.

D.zh: 建立 EKS 叢集。建立一個 IAM 角色，該角色具有向客戶託管金鑰授予權限的策略。將角色與 EKS 叢集關聯。
D.en: Create the EKS cluster. Create an IAM role that has a policy that grants permission to the customer managed key. Associate the role with the EKS cluster.

E.zh: 將客戶管理的金鑰作為 Kubernetes 金鑰儲存在 EKS 叢集中。使用客戶管理的密鑰加密 EBS 磁碟區。
E.en: Store the customer managed key as a Kubernetes secret in the EKS cluster. Use the customer managed key to encrypt the EBS volumes.

A.zh: 錯誤。使用 Kubernetes 插件，該插件使用客戶管理的金鑰執行資料加密。雖然可以使用 Kubernetes 外掛程式來實現加密，但這會增加額外的操作開銷，不符合「最小化操作開銷」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Use a Kubernetes plug-in that performs data encryption using customer-managed keys. Although it is possible to use Kubernetes plugins to implement encryption, this adds additional operational overhead and does not meet the requirement of "minimizing operational overhead".

B.zh: 錯誤。此選項手動定位EBS磁碟區並啟用加密需要額外的步驟，增加了操作開銷，且不符合問題要求。
B.en: Incorrect. After creating the EKS cluster, locate the EBS volumes. Enable encryption using a customer-managed key. Manually locating the EBS volume and enabling encryption requires additional steps, which increases operational overhead and does not meet the question requirements.

C.zh: 正確。在建立 EKS 叢集的 AWS 區域中啟用 EBS 預設加密。選擇客戶管理的密鑰作為預設密鑰。啟用 EBS 預設加密並選擇客戶管理的金鑰作為預設金鑰是一種自動化解決方案，可減少營運開銷。
C.en: Correct. Enable EBS default encryption in the AWS Region where the EKS cluster is created. Select the customer-managed key as the default key. Enabling EBS default encryption and selecting a customer-managed key as the default key is an automated solution that reduces operational overhead.

D.zh: 正確。建立 EKS 叢集。使用授予客戶管理金鑰權限的策略建立 IAM 角色。將角色與 EKS 叢集關聯。為 EKS 叢集建立 IAM 角色並授予對客戶管理金鑰的存取權限是啟用加密而不增加額外操作開銷的必要步驟。
D.en: Correct. Create an EKS cluster. Create an IAM role with a policy that grants customers permission to manage keys. Associate the role with the EKS cluster. Creating an IAM role for the EKS cluster and granting access to customer-managed keys are necessary steps to enable encryption without adding additional operational overhead.

E.zh: 錯誤。此選項將金鑰儲存為 Kubernetes 機密並手動加密 EBS 磁碟區會增加操作複雜性，不符合「最小化操作開銷」的要求。
E.en: Incorrect. Store customer-managed keys as Kubernetes secrets in the EKS cluster. Encrypt EBS volumes using customer-managed keys. Storing keys as Kubernetes secrets and manually encrypting EBS volumes increases operational complexity and does not meet the requirement of "minimizing operational overhead".

answer_text.zh: C. 在將建立 EKS 叢集的 AWS 區域中預設啟用 EBS 加密。選擇客戶管理的密鑰作為預設密鑰。；D. 建立 EKS 叢集。建立一個 IAM 角色，該角色具有向客戶託管金鑰授予權限的策略。將角色與 EKS 叢集關聯。
answer_text.en: C. Enable EBS encryption by default in the AWS Region where the EKS cluster will be created. Select the customer managed key as the default key.; D. Create the EKS cluster. Create an IAM role that has a policy that grants permission to the customer managed key. Associate the role with the EKS cluster.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：CD (53%)； BD (44%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：在建立 EKS 叢集的 AWS 區域中啟用 EBS 預設加密。選擇客戶管理的密鑰作為預設密鑰。啟用 EBS 預設加密並選擇客戶管理的金鑰作為預設金鑰是一種自動化解決方案，可減少營運開銷。；建立 EKS 叢集。使用授予客戶管理金鑰權限的策略建立 IAM 角色。將角色與 EKS 叢集關聯。為 EKS 叢集建立 IAM 角色並授予對客戶管理金鑰的存取權限是啟用加密而不增加額外操作開銷的必要步驟。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: CD (53%); BD (44%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Enable EBS default encryption in the AWS Region where the EKS cluster is created. Select the customer-managed key as the default key. Enabling EBS default encryption and selecting a customer-managed key as the default key is an automated solution that reduces operational overhead.; Create an EKS cluster. Create an IAM role with a policy that grants customers permission to manage keys. Associate the role with the EKS cluster. Creating an IAM role for the EKS cluster and granting access to customer-managed keys are necessary steps to enable encryption without adding additional operational overhead.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司想要將 Oracle 資料庫遷移到 AWS。該資料庫由一個表組成，其中包含數百萬個高解析度的地理資訊系統 (GIS) 影像，並透過地理代碼進行識別。當自然災害發生時，每隔幾分鐘就會更新數萬張影像。每個地理代碼都有一個與其關聯的圖像或行。該公司希望有一個在此類事件期間具有高可用性和可擴展性的解決方案。哪種解決方案最經濟有效率地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to migrate an Oracle database to AWS. The database consists of a single table that contains millions of geographic information systems (GIS) images that are high resolution and are identified by a geographic code. When a natural disaster occurs, tens of thousands of images get updated every few minutes. Each geographic code has a single image or row that is associated with it. The company wants a solution that is highly available and scalable during such events. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 將影像和地理程式碼儲存在資料庫表中。使用在 Amazon RDS 多重使用區資料庫執行個體上執行的 Oracle。
A.en: Store the images and geographic codes in a database table. Use Oracle running on an Amazon RDS Multi-AZ DB instance.

B.zh: 將影像儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。使用 Amazon DynamoDB，將地理代碼作為鍵，將圖像 S3 URL 作為值。
B.en: Store the images in Amazon S3 buckets. Use Amazon DynamoDB with the geographic code as the key and the image S3 URL as the value.

C.zh: 將影像和地理程式碼儲存在 Amazon DynamoDB 表中。在高負載期間配置 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX)。
C.en: Store the images and geographic codes in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Configure DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) during times of high load.

D.zh: 將影像儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。將地理代碼和圖像 S3 URL 儲存在資料庫表中。使用在 Amazon RDS 多重使用區資料庫執行個體上執行的 Oracle。
D.en: Store the images in Amazon S3 buckets. Store geographic codes and image S3 URLs in a database table. Use Oracle running on an Amazon RDS Multi-AZ DB instance.

A.zh: 錯誤。在現成的資料庫（例如 Oracle）中儲存大量高解析度映像會導致儲存成本高且效能不佳。雖然 Amazon RDS 多可用區提供了高可用性，但它不適合儲存大量非結構化資料（例如影像）。
A.en: Incorrect. Storing large amounts of high-resolution images in an off-the-shelf database (such as Oracle) results in expensive storage and poor performance. Although Amazon RDS Multi-AZ provides high availability, it is not suitable for storing large amounts of unstructured data (such as images).

B.zh: 正確。Amazon S3 是一種高效且經濟高效的儲存解決方案，用於儲存大量影像。 Amazon DynamoDB 是一種高度可用且可擴充的 NoSQL 資料庫，適合儲存地理程式碼和映像 URL 的對應。該架構可以在高負載下快速擴展，同時保持成本效益。
B.en: Correct. Amazon S3 is an efficient and cost-effective storage solution for storing large amounts of images. Amazon DynamoDB is a highly available and scalable NoSQL database suitable for storing mappings of geographic codes and image URLs. This architecture scales quickly under high load while remaining cost-effective.

C.zh: 錯誤。DynamoDB 不適合直接儲存大量高解析度影像，因為它的儲存成本更高，且效能不如 S3。雖然DAX可以提高DynamoDB的效能，但它並沒有解決儲存影像的成本。
C.en: Incorrect. DynamoDB is not suitable for directly storing large amounts of high-resolution images because it is more expensive to store and does not perform as well as S3. Although DAX can improve the performance of DynamoDB, it does not solve the cost of storing images.

D.zh: 錯誤。將影像部分儲存在S3是正確的選擇。與 DynamoDB 相比，使用 Oracle 資料庫儲存地理程式碼和映像 URL 的對應會導致更高的成本和更低的效能。
D.en: Incorrect. Partially storing images in S3 is a correct choice. Using Oracle Database to store mappings for geographic codes and image URLs results in higher costs and lower performance than DynamoDB.

answer_text.zh: B. 將影像儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中。使用 Amazon DynamoDB，將地理代碼作為鍵，將圖像 S3 URL 作為值。
answer_text.en: B. Store the images in Amazon S3 buckets. Use Amazon DynamoDB with the geographic code as the key and the image S3 URL as the value.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (72%)； D (28%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Amazon S3 是一種高效且經濟高效的儲存解決方案，用於儲存大量影像。 Amazon DynamoDB 是一種高度可用且可擴充的 NoSQL 資料庫，適合儲存地理程式碼和映像 URL 的對應。該架構可以在高負載下快速擴展，同時保持成本效益。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (72%); D (28%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon S3 is an efficient and cost-effective storage solution for storing large amounts of images. Amazon DynamoDB is a highly available and scalable NoSQL database suitable for storing mappings of geographic codes and image URLs. This architecture scales quickly under high load while remaining cost-effective.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司有一個從汽車上的物聯網感測器收集資料的應用程式。資料透過 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 串流並儲存在 Amazon S3 中。這些數據每年產生數萬億個 S3 物件。每天早上，該公司都會使用過去 30 天的資料重新訓練一套機器學習 (ML) 模型。該公司每年四次使用前 12 個月的數據進行分析並訓練其他機器學習模型。資料的可用時間必須最短，延遲時間最長為 1 年。一年後，資料必須保留以供存檔。哪種儲存解決方案最經濟有效率地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company has an application that collects data from IoT sensors on automobiles. The data is streamed and stored in Amazon S3 through Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose. The data produces trillions of S3 objects each year. Each morning, the company uses the data from the previous 30 days to retrain a suite of machine learning (ML) models. Four times each year, the company uses the data from the previous 12 months to perform analysis and train other ML models. The data must be available with minimal delay for up to 1 year. After 1 year, the data must be retained for archival purposes. Which storage solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 使用 S3 智慧分層儲存類別。創建 S3 生命週期策略以在 1 年後將物件過渡到 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
A.en: Use the S3 Intelligent-Tiering storage class. Create an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year.

B.zh: 使用 S3 智慧分層儲存類別。配置 S3 智慧分層以在 1 年後自動將物件移至 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
B.en: Use the S3 Intelligent-Tiering storage class. Configure S3 Intelligent-Tiering to automatically move objects to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year.

C.zh: 使用 S3 標準-不頻繁存取（S3 標準-IA）儲存類別。創建 S3 生命週期策略以在 1 年後將物件過渡到 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
C.en: Use the S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) storage class. Create an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year.

D.zh: 使用 S3 標準儲存類別。建立 S3 生命週期策略，以在 30 天後將物件轉換為 S3 標準-不頻繁存取 (S3 標準-IA)，然後在 1 年後將物件轉換為 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
D.en: Use the S3 Standard storage class. Create an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) after 30 days, and then to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year.

A.zh: 錯誤。S3智慧分層適用於資料存取方式不確定的場景，但其成本低於S3 Standard-IA，且生命週期策略與智慧分層的自動分層功能重疊。無需使用 S3 智慧分層，因為資料存取模式很明確（30 天訪問，此後訪問較少）。
A.en: Incorrect. S3 Intelligent-Tiering is suitable for scenarios where the data access mode is uncertain, but its cost is lower than S3 Standard-IA, and the life cycle strategy overlaps with the automatic tiering function of Intelligent-Tiering. There is no need to use S3 Intelligent-Tiering because the data access pattern is clear (30 days of access, less access after that).

B.zh: 錯誤。S3 智慧分層可以自動分層，但其成本低於 S3 Standard-IA，無法直接設定為將資料傳輸至 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。 S3智慧分層不支援直接傳輸到S3 Glacier Deep Archive，成本優化不夠。
B.en: Incorrect. S3 Intelligent-Tiering can automatically tier, but its cost is lower than S3 Standard-IA, and it cannot be directly configured to transfer data to S3 Glacier Deep Archive. S3 Intelligent-Tiering does not support direct transfer to S3 Glacier Deep Archive, and the cost is not optimized enough.

C.zh: 錯誤。S3 Standard-IA 適用於訪問量較少的數據，但沒有考慮前 30 天內的訪問需求。直接使用S3 Standard-IA會導致前30天內的訪問成本增加。前 30 天的儲存成本未最佳化，S3 標準儲存類別未充分利用。
C.en: Incorrect. S3 Standard-IA is suitable for less accessed data, but it does not consider the need for access in the first 30 days. Direct use of S3 Standard-IA will lead to an increase in access costs in the first 30 days. The storage cost of the first 30 days was not optimized and the S3 Standard storage category was not fully utilized.

D.zh: 正確。S3 Standard 適用於前 30 天內存取的數據，S3 Standard-IA 適用於訪問量較少的數據，S3 Glacier Deep Archive 適用於後期歸檔的數據。此方案充分利用S3儲存的特點，並採用生命週期策略來實現成本最佳化。
D.en: Correct. S3 Standard is suitable for data accessed in the first 30 days, S3 Standard-IA is suitable for less accessed data, and S3 Glacier Deep Archive is suitable for later archived data. This option makes full use of the characteristics of S3 storage and adopts a life cycle strategy to achieve cost optimization.

answer_text.zh: D. 使用 S3 標準儲存類別。建立 S3 生命週期策略，以在 30 天後將物件轉換為 S3 標準-不頻繁存取 (S3 標準-IA)，然後在 1 年後將物件轉換為 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
answer_text.en: D. Use the S3 Standard storage class. Create an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) after 30 days, and then to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (91%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：S3 Standard 適用於前 30 天內存取的數據，S3 Standard-IA 適用於訪問量較少的數據，S3 Glacier Deep Archive 適用於後期歸檔的數據。此方案充分利用S3儲存的特點，並採用生命週期策略來實現成本最佳化。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (91%). Popular discussion and key point summary: S3 Standard is suitable for data accessed in the first 30 days, S3 Standard-IA is suitable for less accessed data, and S3 Glacier Deep Archive is suitable for later archived data. This option makes full use of the characteristics of S3 storage and adopts a life cycle strategy to achieve cost optimization.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在 us-east-1 區域內的三個獨立的 VPC 中運行多個業務應用程式。應用程式必須能夠在 VPC 之間進行通訊。應用程式還必須能夠每天持續將數百 GB 的資料傳送到在單一本地資料中心運行的延遲敏感型應用程式。解決方案架構師需要設計一個能夠最大限度提高成本效益的網路連接解決方案。哪種解決方案滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is running several business applications in three separate VPCs within the us-east-1 Region. The applications must be able to communicate between VPCs. The applications also must be able to consistently send hundreds of gigabytes of data each day to a latency-sensitive application that runs in a single on-premises data center. A solutions architect needs to design a network connectivity solution that maximizes cost-effectiveness. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 設定從資料中心到 AWS 的三個 AWS Site-to-Site VPN 連線。透過為每個 VPC 設定一個 VPN 連線來建立連線。
A.en: Configure three AWS Site-to-Site VPN connections from the data center to AWS. Establish connectivity by configuring one VPN connection for each VPC.

B.zh: 在每個 VPC 中啟動第三方虛擬網路設備。在資料中心和每個虛擬設備之間建立 IPsec VPN 隧道。
B.en: Launch a third-party virtual network appliance in each VPC. Establish an IPsec VPN tunnel between the data center and each virtual appliance.

C.zh: 設定從資料中心到 us-east-1 中的 Direct Connect 閘道的三個 AWS Direct Connect 連線。透過將每個 VPC 設定為使用 Direct Connect 連線之一來建立連線。
C.en: Set up three AWS Direct Connect connections from the data center to a Direct Connect gateway in us-east-1. Establish connectivity by configuring each VPC to use one of the Direct Connect connections.

D.zh: 設定一個從資料中心到 AWS 的 AWS Direct Connect 連線。建立中轉網關，並將每個 VPC 附加到中轉網關。在 Direct Connect 連線和中轉網關之間建立連線。
D.en: Set up one AWS Direct Connect connection from the data center to AWS. Create a transit gateway, and attach each VPC to the transit gateway. Establish connectivity between the Direct Connect connection and the transit gateway.

A.zh: 錯誤。配置三個 AWS Site-to-Site VPN 連接：儘管 Site-to-Site VPN 可以實現連接，但它是基於 Internet 的解決方案，可能會導致嚴重的延遲和不穩定，特別是對於每天傳輸數百 GB 資料的延遲敏感應用程式。從成本上來說，VPN的成本較低，但效能無法滿足需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Configuring three AWS Site-to-Site VPN connections: Although Site-to-Site VPN can achieve connectivity, it is an Internet-based solution that can cause significant latency and instability, especially for latency-sensitive applications that transfer hundreds of gigabytes of data per day. In terms of cost, the cost of VPN is lower, but the performance cannot meet the needs.

B.zh: 錯誤。啟動第三方虛擬網路設備並建立IPsec VPN隧道：這種方法很複雜，並且需要額外的管理開銷。第三方設備成本較高，且仍基於網際網路連接，無法保證低延遲和高吞吐量。不符合「成本效益最大化」的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Launching a third-party virtual network device and establishing an IPsec VPN tunnel: This method is complex and requires additional management overhead. The cost of third-party equipment is relatively high, and it is still based on Internet connection, which cannot guarantee low latency and high throughput. Does not meet the requirement of "maximizing cost-effectiveness".

C.zh: 錯誤。設定三個 AWS Direct Connect 連線：雖然 Direct Connect 提供低延遲和高吞吐量，但設定三個獨立的 Direct Connect 連線會顯著增加成本，並且無法滿足「最大化成本效益」的要求。專線網關可以支援多個VPC連接，因此無需為每個VPC設定單獨的連接。
C.en: Incorrect. Setting up three AWS Direct Connect connections: Although Direct Connect provides low latency and high throughput, setting up three independent Direct Connect connections significantly increases costs and does not meet the "maximize cost-effectiveness" requirement. Direct Connect Gateway can support multiple VPC connections, so there is no need to set up separate connections for each VPC.

D.zh: 正確。設定 AWS Direct Connect 連線並使用 Transit Gateway：Direct Connect 提供低延遲和吞吐量，適合每天傳輸大量資料的場景。 Transit Gateway 可連接多個 VPC，而 Direct Connect Gateway 可用於連接本機資料中心。該架構在滿足效能要求的同時，簡化了管理並降低了成本。滿足「成本效益最大化」的要求。
D.en: Correct. Set up an AWS Direct Connect connection and use Transit Gateway: Direct Connect provides low latency and throughput, making it suitable for scenarios where large amounts of data are transferred every day. Transit Gateway can connect to multiple VPCs, and Direct Connect Gateway can be used to connect to local data centers. This architecture simplifies management and reduces costs while meeting performance requirements. Meet the requirement of "maximizing cost-effectiveness".

answer_text.zh: D. 設定一個從資料中心到 AWS 的 AWS Direct Connect 連線。建立中轉網關，並將每個 VPC 附加到中轉網關。在 Direct Connect 連線和中轉網關之間建立連線。
answer_text.en: D. Set up one AWS Direct Connect connection from the data center to AWS. Create a transit gateway, and attach each VPC to the transit gateway. Establish connectivity between the Direct Connect connection and the transit gateway.

Transit Gateway can connect to multiple VPCs, and Direct Connect Gateway can be used to connect to local data centers. This architecture simplifies management and reduces costs while meeting performance requirements. Meet the requirement of "maximizing cost-effectiveness".

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家電子商務公司正在建立一個分散式應用程序，該應用程式涉及多個無伺服器功能和 AWS 服務來完成訂單處理任務。作為工作流程的一部分，這些任務需要手動批准。解決方案架構師需要為訂單處理應用程式設計一個架構。此解決方案必須能夠將多個 AWS Lambda 函數組合到響應式無伺服器應用程式中。此解決方案還必須編排在 Amazon EC2 執行個體、容器或本機伺服器上執行的資料和服務。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: An ecommerce company is building a distributed application that involves several serverless functions and AWS services to complete order-processing tasks. These tasks require manual approvals as part of the workflow. A solutions architect needs to design an architecture for the order-processing application. The solution must be able to combine multiple AWS Lambda functions into responsive serverless applications. The solution also must orchestrate data and services that run on Amazon EC2 instances, containers, or on-premises servers. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Step Functions 建置應用程式。
A.en: Use AWS Step Functions to build the application.

B.zh: 將所有應用程式元件整合到 AWS Glue 作業中。
B.en: Integrate all the application components in an AWS Glue job.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 建立應用程式。
C.en: Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) to build the application.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Lambda 函數和 Amazon EventBridge 事件建立應用程式。
D.en: Use AWS Lambda functions and Amazon EventBridge events to build the application.

A.zh: 正確。AWS Step Functions 是一種與伺服器無關的編排服務，可輕鬆地將多個 AWS Lambda 函數和其他服務組合到應用程式中。 Step Functions 支援自動化批次處理，並且可以與 EC2 執行個體、主機和本機伺服器整合。它提供視覺化工作流程設計和狀態管理，減少營運開銷。
A.en: Correct. AWS Step Functions is a server-agnostic orchestration service that makes it easy to combine multiple AWS Lambda functions and other services into applications. Step Functions supports automated batch processes and can be integrated with EC2 instances, hosts, and local servers. It provides visual workflow design and status management, reducing operational overhead.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS Glue 主要用於 ETL（提取、轉換、載入）任務，適合處理資料轉換和分析，而不是編排和分發應用程式。不支援靈活的批次處理，不適合跨服務編排。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS Glue is mainly used for ETL (extract, transform, load) tasks and is suitable for processing data conversion and analysis, rather than orchestrating and distributing applications. It does not support flexible batch processes and is not suitable for cross-service orchestration.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon SQS 是一種解耦佇列服務，適用於解耦元件和非同步通信，但不支援複雜的工作流編排。自動化批次和跨服務編排需要額外的邏輯和服務支持，從而增加營運開銷。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon SQS is a decoupling queue service that is suitable for decoupled components and asynchronous communication, but does not support complex workflow orchestration. Automating batch processes and cross-service orchestration requires additional logic and service support, increasing operational overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然 Lambda 和 EventBridge 可以處理事件驅動的架構，但它們不提供內建的工作流程編排功能。與批次流程互通需要額外的開發工作並增加營運開銷。
D.en: Incorrect. While Lambda and EventBridge can handle event-driven architectures, they do not provide built-in workflow orchestration capabilities. Interoperating with the batch process requires additional development work and increases operational overhead.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 AWS Step Functions 建置應用程式。
answer_text.en: A. Use AWS Step Functions to build the application.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS Step Functions 是一種與伺服器無關的編排服務，可輕鬆地將多個 AWS Lambda 函數和其他服務組合到應用程式中。 Step Functions 支援自動化批次處理，並且可以與 EC2 執行個體、主機和本機伺服器整合。它提供視覺化工作流程設計和狀態管理，減少營運開銷。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Step Functions is a server-agnostic orchestration service that makes it easy to combine multiple AWS Lambda functions and other services into applications. Step Functions supports automated batch processes and can be integrated with EC2 instances, hosts, and local servers. It provides visual workflow design and status management, reducing operational overhead.
